  • Uses of Notation in calculator

    Subclasses with type arguments of type Notation in calculator
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    enum 
    Notation
    Enumeration of the 3 ways to represent an arithmetic expression as a String:
    Fields in calculator declared as Notation
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    Notation
    Operation.notation
    The notation used to render operations as strings.
    Methods in calculator that return Notation
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static Notation
    Notation.valueOf(String name)
    Returns the enum constant of this class with the specified name.
    static Notation[]
    Notation.values()
    Returns an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared.
    Constructors in calculator with parameters of type Notation
    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
     
    Divides(List<Expression> elist, Notation n)
    Class constructor specifying a number of Expressions to divide, as well as the notation used to represent the operation.
     
    Minus(List<Expression> elist, Notation n)
    Class constructor specifying a number of Expressions to subtract, as well as the Notation used to represent the operation.
    protected
    Operation(List<Expression> elist, Notation n)
    To construct an operation with a list of expressions as arguments, as well as the Notation used to represent the operation.
     
    Plus(List<Expression> elist, Notation n)
    Class constructor specifying a number of Expressions to add, as well as the Notation used to represent the operation.
     
    Times(List<Expression> elist, Notation n)
    Class constructor specifying a number of Expressions to multiply, as well as the Notation used to represent the operation.
